name: wsl
description: Interact with WSL distributions from Claude: run commands, read and write files, inspect distros.
long_description:
Gives Claude full access to your Windows Subsystem for Linux environment. Run shell commands, read and write files anywhere on the WSL filesystem, list directory contents, and inspect distribution details — all without leaving a Claude conversation.

Supports multiple distributions: each tool accepts an optional `distro` parameter so you can target Ubuntu, Debian, or any other installed distribution by name. When omitted, tools default to your system default distribution.

**Included tools**
- `list_distros` — see all installed distributions, their running state, and WSL version
- `run_command` — execute any bash command with configurable timeout
- `run_privileged_command` — run a command as root (requires explicit user confirmation before Claude calls it)
- `read_file` — read text files from the WSL filesystem
- `write_file` — create or overwrite files in the WSL filesystem
- `list_directory` — list directory contents with permissions, sizes, and timestamps
- `get_distro_info` — kernel version, OS release details, and current user identity

tools.name: list_distros
tools.description: List all installed WSL distributions, their running state (Running/Stopped), WSL version (1 or 2), and which is the default.

tools.name: run_command
tools.description: Execute a bash command inside a WSL distribution. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code. Supports pipes, scripts, and a configurable timeout. Do not use sudo — use run_privileged_command instead.

tools.name: run_privileged_command
tools.description: ⚠️ ELEVATED — runs as root via wsl.exe --user root. Claude MUST show the user the exact command and ask for explicit confirmation before calling this tool.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a text file from the WSL filesystem by absolute path. Returns the file content as a UTF-8 string.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write or overwrite a file in the WSL filesystem. Creates the file if it does not exist. Parent directory must already exist.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List the contents of a WSL directory with file names, types (file/directory/symlink), Unix permissions, sizes, and last-modified timestamps.

tools.name: get_distro_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a WSL distribution: WSL version, running state, Linux kernel version, OS release details, and current user identity.
